name: Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir Trail
name_original: El Embalse de Navalperal de Pinares desde La Cañada — circular
description: Hike an easy 4.4-mile (7.1 km) circular route around the Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir, enjoying forests and views of Sierra de Gredos.
region_intro: Hiking around Herradón De Pinares offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by pine forests and open landscapes. The region features gentle hills and includes trails around water bodies like reservoirs. Historical elements, such as dolmens, are also present along some paths. Best hiking trails around Herradón De Pinares The most popular hiking route is Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir Trail, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the perimeter of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cuerda de los Polvisos — circular desde San Bartolomé de Pinares, a difficult 13.1 miles (21.0 km) path. This circular route offers significant elevation gain through the landscape. Local hikers also love the Dolmen del Prado de las Cruces Trail, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail leading through historical sites, often completed in about 46 minutes. Hiking around Herradón De Pinares is defined by pine forests, reservoir views, and historical dolmens. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Herradón De Pinares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 5 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Herradón De Pinares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Herradón De Pinares?
A: There are 11 hiking trails documented in Herradón De Pinares, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find 6 easy routes, 3 moderate, and 2 difficult ones.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Herradón De Pinares?
A: Yes, Herradón De Pinares offers several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326180807/dolmen-del-prado-de-las-cruces-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Prado de las Cruces Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is only 2.9 km long and takes about 46 minutes to complete, leading through historical sites.

Q: What are the best circular walks around Herradón De Pinares?
A: Herradón De Pinares features several excellent circular routes. For a challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146097644/cuerda-de-los-polvisos-circular-desde-san-bartolome-de-pinares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda de los Polvisos — circular desde San Bartolomé de Pinares&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 21 km path. A moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326136347/bernuy-salinero-from-urraca-miguel-circular-by-the-camino-natural-de-campo-azalvaro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bernuy-Salinero from Urraca Miguel — circular by the Camino Natural de Campo Azálvaro&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 14.6 km.

Q: Are there any short loop trails for a quick walk?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326180807/dolmen-del-prado-de-las-cruces-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Prado de las Cruces Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a short, easy loop of 2.9 km, perfect for a quick walk. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326284678/navalperal-de-pinares-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.1 km and takes under two hours.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes in Herradón De Pinares?
A: Hikes in Herradón De Pinares offer diverse scenery, including pine forests, open landscapes, and views of water bodies like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find panoramic vistas from spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5621589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Canchal&lt;/a&gt; or the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2879433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Cabeza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region is home to historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326180807/dolmen-del-prado-de-las-cruces-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Prado de las Cruces Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads to the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Prado de Las Cruces&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Herradón De Pinares?
A: The trails in Herradón De Pinares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from pine forests to reservoir views, and the options available for all ability levels.

Q: Is Herradón De Pinares suitable for beginner hikers?
A: Yes, Herradón De Pinares has several easy routes perfect for beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326284678/navalperal-de-pinares-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, being an easy 7.1 km path that follows the perimeter of the reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Herradón De Pinares?
A: The region's varied terrain, including pine forests and open landscapes, makes it enjoyable across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winter can provide a different, quieter experience, especially in the pine forests.

Q: Are there any trails with significant elevation gain for a more challenging hike?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146097644/cuerda-de-los-polvisos-circular-desde-san-bartolome-de-pinares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda de los Polvisos — circular desde San Bartolomé de Pinares&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21 km route with significant elevation gain, offering a strenuous workout and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: While specific cafes directly on the trails are not listed, the nearby settlements like Herradón De Pinares itself or San Bartolomé de Pinares would be the best places to find local establishments for refreshments after your hike.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Herradón De Pinares?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for different weather conditions, it's advisable to wear comfortable, layered clothing suitable for hiking. Sturdy hiking boots are recommended, especially for routes with uneven paths or elevation changes. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.
tips: The Casasola Route in Navalperal de Pinares is one of the longest and most popular hiking trails in the area. With a length of approximately 23 kilometers and a cumulative ascent of 346 meters, this route offers a complete experience for hiking enthusiasts. The route begins at the Hermitage of San Antonio and passes through diverse landscapes, including oak forests, reforested pine forests, and mountain meadows.

The Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir is a beautiful place located in the province of Ávila, ideal for enjoying nature and outdoor activities. Built in 1980, this reservoir is located on the Maillo Stream.

It is a perfect destination for relaxation and recreation, offering activities such as fishing, picnicking, and hiking. Furthermore, the surrounding natural environment offers peaceful and enchanting landscapes, ideal for unwinding and enjoying the serenity of the place.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir from La Cañada — circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail begins in La Cañada. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as a popular and easily accessible circular route, you can typically find parking near the trailhead in La Cañada. It's always a good idea to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This route is classified as an easy hike, making it ideal for all fitness levels, including beginners and families. The paths are generally accessible, and the modest elevation gain ensures a comfortable experience for everyone.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse and relaxing landscape. The trail winds through beautiful pine forests (pinares), oak forests (robles), and mountain pastures (prados montanos). The highlight is undoubtedly the serene Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir, offering peaceful views. You'll also get expansive vistas of the Sierra de Gredos.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions are the tranquil Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir itself, and the scenic Casasola Route (Navalperal de Pinares). The combination of forests, pastures, and reservoir views provides a rich variety of natural beauty throughout the hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer, while winter provides a different, often quieter, experience.

faqs.question: Is the Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir from La Cañada — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. It's a great option for enjoying nature with your canine companion. Please remember to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to hike the Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir from La Cañada — circular trail. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is approximately 7.06 kilometers (4.39 miles) long and typically takes about 1 hour and 54 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. This duration allows ample time to enjoy the scenery without feeling rushed.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of the historic Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental at several points. This ancient drovers' road forms a significant portion of the trail, adding to its character.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this easy circular hike, we recommend bringing comfortable walking shoes, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a camera to capture the beautiful scenery. Depending on the season, a light jacket might also be useful.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The area around the Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir is known for recreation, including picnicking. While specific cafes or pubs directly at the trailhead aren't highlighted, Navalperal de Pinares and La Cañada are small towns, so basic amenities may be found nearby.
